sources.title: From Batch to Buyer: Designing More Traceable Pharmaceutical Supply Chains
sources.capabilityBoundary: Educational corporate content only. This article describes design principles and NovaPharm's traceability roadmap. It does not claim a deployed blockchain system or replace applicable legal, GDP, safety-feature or recall requirements.

sources.title: Building a Compliance-First Pharmaceutical Distribution Model in the UK
sources.capabilityBoundary: Educational corporate content only. This article is not legal, regulatory, clinical or licensing advice. Organisations should obtain advice appropriate to their products, activities and authorisations.

sources.title: GDP, QMS and the Practical Foundations of Reliable Pharmaceutical Distribution
sources.capabilityBoundary: Educational corporate content only. GDP and quality-system requirements depend on an organisation's authorised activities, products, sites and outsourcing model. Use current official guidance and qualified advice.

sources.title: Why Oncology Supply Chains Require Specialised Demand Forecasting
sources.capabilityBoundary: Educational corporate content only. This article does not provide clinical advice and does not describe a deployed NovaPharm forecasting service. Any future model will require validated data, governance and expert oversight.

sources.title: What PLPI Means for Pharmaceutical Supply Resilience and Cost-Optimised Access
sources.capabilityBoundary: Educational corporate content only. PLPI eligibility, evidence, fees and obligations are product-specific and may change. Refer to current MHRA guidance and obtain qualified advice before acting.

sources.title: Three-Pillar Sourcing: Direct GMP Partnerships, PLPI and European Buying Networks
sources.capabilityBoundary: Educational corporate content only. The three-pillar model describes NovaPharm's strategy and development roadmap. It does not confirm a supplier, product licence, inventory position or medicine availability.
